Great Hearts, Noble Hearts
gameplay / military-off-season
Jules Lavelle 8/11/2020 1:27 PM
Apply to join a regiment by choosing one from Regimental Table A, which lists regiments in descending order of prestige. On application, roll 1d6; if the number is equal to or greater than the requirement for the character's social level, the application is accepted and the character is granted the rank of Private. The character may immediately choose to purchase a commission, if he is of the required social level, can afford it, and there is an open position for that rank in the regiment. Purchasing a commission costs crowns equal to that rank plus all the intervening ranks. Lieutenant-Colonel and above ranks cannot be purchased at character creation, regardless of meeting the requirements. Members of cavalry regiments and captains in infantry regiments must purchase a horse. Majors and above in any regiment must buy three horses. A horse costs 100 crowns, and each horse requires 3 crowns per month to keep, plus 2 crowns per month to pay a groom (who can care for any number of horses). If the character's application is rejected, he may immediately apply to join another regiment in the same week, but only ones of lower standing. For every two rejections, subtract one from the die roll requirement for subsequent applications. Regiments will be generated as they are joined by players. ============================= Privates are required to spend two weeks of each month performing regimental duties. Subalterns are required to spend one week each month. These duties are not necessary the month of enlistment. Captains and above have no regimental duties while off campaign. Characters may request a week off by rolling 5 or 6 on a 1d6. This may be modified by influence. (edited)
Jules Lavelle pinned a message to this channel. 8/11/2020 1:39 PM

Dice Golem BOT 8/22/2020 10:31 AM
Dice Golem Help
I roll dice! I respond to /roll commands like /roll d20. /roll info provides more information.
Examples
/roll 2d20 + 1 - Roll two D20s and add 1. Basic math operators like -+*/% are also supported. /roll 3dF - Roll 3 Fate/Fudge dice. /roll 4d6d1 - Roll four D6s and drop the lowest one. Also does keeps/k, drop highest/dh and keep lowest/kl. /roll 2d20kl1 - Simulate disadvantage by keeping the lowest result out of two D20s. /roll 2d20r1 - Roll two D20s, re-rolling any 1s. Can also reroll by comparisons, ex. 2d20r<3. /roll d20ro1 - Roll a D20 and re-roll it only once if the result was a 1. Comparison checks work here too! /roll 8d6s - Roll 8 D6s and sort the results. Sorts ascending, i.e. sa, by default, but can sort descending/8d6sd. /roll 3d6 # Fire damage - Add a comment to a roll after a # or \. /roll round(d420/42) - Functions max, min, ceil, floor, round and abs supported.
Narrator 8/22/2020 10:31 AM
you just need a 1d6

Narrator 8/22/2020 10:59 AM
@Abelard Soucy , @Ghôst Touchard , you are now members of Enemy Regiments. Should you run into one another, there will be Cause for a Duel.
Ghôst Touchard 8/22/2020 11:00 AM
Huh, like at the club at the same time?
Narrator 8/22/2020 11:02 AM
yes, that's really the only way you could happen to meet, other than agreeing to meet other people for a duel in the same place at the same time
11:02 AM
passing on the street alone wouldn't be enough -- you need to be in the same place for a while for the regimental rivalry to lead to an insult
Abelard Soucy 8/22/2020 11:03 AM
As if having competing family businesses wasn't enough!
Ghôst Touchard 8/22/2020 11:03 AM
Well this should shake things up a bit. Fortunately I am a private, so we may not run into each other so often
Narrator 8/22/2020 11:06 AM
@Anonymouse , @Ghôst Touchard , you are now members of Friendly Regiments. Should one of you overhear an insult towards the other, there will be Cause for a Duel with the slanderer in defense of your friend's honor.
Ghôst Touchard 8/22/2020 11:07 AM
@Ghôst Touchard, @Dice Golem, you are now members of Friendly Regiments.
3
Narrator 8/22/2020 11:24 AM
to be clear; the Friendly Regiment Cause will not occur if the insult is made to your Friends' face. in that case it's up to the Friend to defend his own honor
11:27 AM
and naturally, the same Cause will exist if you overhear an insult directed towards someone in the same regiment as you
Ghôst Touchard 8/22/2020 11:28 AM
So if you make plans to toady.. and that person enlists in an enemy regiment earlier that month...
Jules Lavelle 8/22/2020 11:28 AM
wow, it's like you're not even describing a hypothetical, but an exact situation that is going to come to a head on week 3!
1
Ghôst Touchard 8/22/2020 11:30 AM

1
Jules Lavelle 8/22/2020 11:31 AM
actually they don't care about each other
Ghôst Touchard 8/22/2020 11:31 AM
Well there's that at least
11:34 AM
And what of QOC and the Grand Duke Max? Or is it too soon to reveal?
Jules Lavelle 8/22/2020 11:38 AM
regimental relationships are in regimental table C, it's not a secret
11:38 AM
grand duke max is friends with the dragoon guards
11:38 AM
each regiment has one friend and one enemy, except the cardinal's guard, which has no friends
11:38 AM
and the royal foot guards, which has no enemies
Ghôst Touchard 8/22/2020 11:39 AM

11:39 AM

harry 8/22/2020 12:44 PM
Ah, but which regiment may I join where my money is any good?
Narrator 8/22/2020 12:48 PM
money is always good to buy a commission, but you do not qualify for an officer's rank in the dragoon guards or above
12:50 PM
the cardinal's guard, the queen's own carabiniers, and the 53rd fusiliers are the only ones that were generated before this month. anything else is fair game
12:52 PM
you could still buy a subaltern rank in any of those, there's no limit there
Narrator 8/22/2020 1:02 PM
were you planning to buy a captaincy in the QOC? that would have cost you 230, plus 100 for the horse. incidentally you are not eligible for a captaincy in any higher ranked regiments, so if you had the money for QOC you have the money for anything else you qualify for
1:03 PM
in the meantime! @Jules Lavelle , roll to join the Royal Marines. You should be aiming for a 5.

harry 8/22/2020 3:11 PM

harry 8/22/2020 3:11 PM
Ah, calvary again, right - not a problem, I'm good for it
Narrator 8/22/2020 3:12 PM
captains always need a horse so it doesn't matter whether you're cavalry or not
harry 8/22/2020 3:12 PM
Ah
Narrator 8/22/2020 3:12 PM
Congratulations to the new junior captain in Princess Louisa's Light Dragoons!

Curtis Sinnoch 11/6/2020 11:03 AM
Ah, the feel of the wind! The air itself! It stirs me and my men to action! Farewell, Paris, for @Curtis Sinnoch marches to war!
1
Narrator 11/10/2020 12:08 AM
Now is the time to apply for military appointments, if desired.
Narrator 11/10/2020 12:17 AM
The deadline to apply will be tomorrow evening, after which uninfluenced commanders will make their own appointments & will decide whether to volunteer for the fall campaign season.
Blastagarded 11/10/2020 12:59 AM
Do we PM you the application(s)?
Jules Lavelle 11/10/2020 1:00 AM
you can just roll for it here, with whatever influence modifiers you want to use
Blastagarded 11/10/2020 1:01 AM
Just military appointments, not government too?
Jules Lavelle 11/10/2020 1:01 AM
you can roll for govt appointments whenever you want, military can only be gotten at the end of summer
Blastagarded 11/10/2020 1:07 AM
I would like to first make an attempt at Commissioner of Public Safety
Narrator 11/10/2020 1:10 AM
The base die roll is an 8, and you subtract 1 for being SL 10. Do you have a level 7 Favour to influence the Minister of State to bring it lower?
Blastagarded 11/10/2020 1:15 AM
I don't believe so, I think I have at most level 5 (2 from SL 10 and 3 from Mistress).
1:16 AM
Unless I can't add them to get... but it's moot anyway.
1:16 AM
So I'm looking for a 7 or better?
Narrator 11/10/2020 1:16 AM
Yes, on a 1d6
Blastagarded 11/10/2020 1:16 AM
Er... nevermind then...
Narrator 11/10/2020 1:16 AM
those favors don't add together, 2 favors at one level are equal to 1 favor at the next level up
1:17 AM
one option is to make deals with other players and get them to share influence with you
Blastagarded 11/10/2020 1:17 AM
Nah, we'll get there eventually. I am eligible for Aide to General, correct?
1:18 AM
A 6 on a 1d6?
Narrator 11/10/2020 1:18 AM
yes. the base roll for you would be a 6
1:19 AM
keep in mind, as a general's aide you cannot volunteer
Blastagarded 11/10/2020 1:19 AM
But I get a level 3 inf, correct?
Narrator 11/10/2020 1:19 AM
yes, per season
Blastagarded 11/10/2020 1:19 AM
Well, here we go
1:20 AM
/roll 1d6
Dice Golem BOT 11/10/2020 1:20 AM
​Hercule Bonaparte rolled 1d6: (5) = 5
1
Blastagarded 11/10/2020 1:21 AM
What's the military ability special requirement?
Narrator 11/10/2020 1:22 AM
you get a modifier to the application roll based on your MA
1:22 AM
6-7 is a -1
1:23 AM
8+ is a -2
1:23 AM
1 is a +1
Blastagarded 11/10/2020 1:23 AM
Well, might as well go for Brigade Major.
1:23 AM
So that's base 6 - 1 for SL - 1 for MA for net 4 on a d6?
Narrator 11/10/2020 1:24 AM
it's -2 for SL. you get a -1 for every 3 SL over the req, which is 3
Blastagarded 11/10/2020 1:25 AM
1:25 AM
/roll 1d6
Dice Golem BOT 11/10/2020 1:25 AM
​Hercule Bonaparte rolled 1d6: (3) = 3
Blastagarded 11/10/2020 1:25 AM
whew Looks like I needed it too (edited)
Narrator 11/10/2020 1:25 AM
You have been appointed the Brigade Major for the Heavy Brigade.
Blastagarded 11/10/2020 1:26 AM
Blastagarded 11/10/2020 12:22 PM
Possible rub, how does being a Brigade Major now impact applying to other regiments?
Narrator 11/10/2020 12:29 PM
It doesn't
12:30 PM
you don't technically serve with your regiment while a brigade major anyway. just don't let the brigadier general find out you ditched his brigade
Curtis Sinnoch 11/10/2020 1:31 PM
May I roll as well for a Brigade Major?
1:31 PM
I believe I need a 3+ as well
Jules Lavelle 11/10/2020 1:32 PM
yes
Curtis Sinnoch 11/10/2020 1:34 PM
And level inf is needed? Also, aren't NPC granted INF only done once a year, not once a season?
Narrator 11/10/2020 1:46 PM
if you wanted to influence a brigadier general, you would need a level 4 favor
Curtis Sinnoch 11/10/2020 1:47 PM
I'll burn a L4 for this roll
1:47 PM
putting me at a 2+
1:48 PM
(Right? -2 SL, -1 MA, -1 L4 Infl)
Narrator 11/10/2020 1:49 PM
yes
Curtis Sinnoch 11/10/2020 1:49 PM
/roll 1d6
Dice Golem BOT 11/10/2020 1:49 PM
​Curtis Sinnoch rolled 1d6: (2) = 2
Narrator 11/10/2020 1:50 PM
to clarify the influence rules: Favors you earn seasonally from your SL (Influence Table A) can be banked indefinitely. Favors you earn seasonally from your appointment can be banked as long as you hold the appointment. If unused, they are lost when you leave the appoint. Re-upping will preserve banked favors. Favors you earn yearly from your mistress cannot be banked. They are also lost if you lose your mistress.
1
1:54 PM

Anonymouse 11/10/2020 2:03 PM

Blastagarded 11/10/2020 2:04 PM
to clarify the influence rules: Favors you earn seasonally from your SL (Influence Table A) can be banked indefinitely. Favors you earn seasonally from your appointment can be banked as long as you hold the appointment. If unused, they are lost when you leave the appoint. Re-upping will preserve banked favors.
@Narrator Rules on OneNote say use-or-lose for these. We should be tracking our SL favors then? Piling them up like a dragon's hoard?
Narrator 11/10/2020 2:04 PM
@Anonymouse do you mean you're sending them but not going yourself? that is an option
2:04 PM
@Blastagarded correct this is a modification to the rules
2:05 PM
so you will need to track them
Ghôst Touchard 11/10/2020 2:05 PM
Anonymouse 11/10/2020 2:05 PM

Jean Renault 11/10/2020 2:07 PM

Anonymouse 11/10/2020 2:08 PM

Jules Lavelle 11/15/2020 11:50 AM

11:52 AM
A step down, yes. But a lower rung on a taller ladder.
Narrator 11/15/2020 11:54 AM
@Curtis Sinnoch and @Blastagarded arrive at the Royal Foot Guards' barracks early the same morning. Each is seen to breathe a sigh of relief when Jules accepts his Captaincy. But their Major's epaulettes gleam golden.
11:55 AM
Assuming you would each like to purchase a Majority, and being as there's only one available, whoever gets the higher application roll will have first dibs on the Majority.
11:56 AM
If you lose but still are accepted to the regiment, you would not be forced to join if you'd rather not.
Blastagarded 11/15/2020 11:56 AM
/roll 1d6
Dice Golem BOT 11/15/2020 11:56 AM
​Hercule Bonaparte rolled 1d6: (3) = 3
Narrator 11/15/2020 11:57 AM
Hercule has been turned away. The Royal Foot are interested in those with pedigree.
11:58 AM
(unless you have influence you'd like to use)
11:58 AM
a level 3 favour would be sufficient
Blastagarded 11/15/2020 12:01 PM
Fortunate for Hercule that Alida is an old family friend of the recruiter
Narrator 11/15/2020 12:02 PM
And of course, more influence could be used to improve your chances against Curtis for the Majority.
1
Blastagarded 11/15/2020 12:04 PM
The benefit of Hercule's beau is done without his knowledge. In his mind, this application is on merit and talent alone.
Narrator 11/15/2020 12:18 PM
As Hercule limps away from the command office, Germain Simond, the acting commander, flings the door open. "Wait there!" He shouts. "I forgot the...ah...the cane bonus. Yes. The cane adds dignity. The Royal Foot Guards...prize...dignity. Come back in, Sir!" He hurriedly tucks a perfumed letter back into his vest pocket.
3
Blastagarded 11/15/2020 12:21 PM
A perplexed Hercule returns to office... subtlety adding a small flourish to the movement of his cane, still somewhat confused.
1
Curtis Sinnoch 11/15/2020 12:40 PM
Ah, well, a cane bonus! That, of course, cannot be understated!
12:41 PM
But, what is a cane against the military might and family name of the Sinnochs!
12:41 PM
/roll 1d6
Dice Golem BOT 11/15/2020 12:41 PM
​Curtis Sinnoch rolled 1d6: (2) = 2
Narrator 11/15/2020 12:42 PM
Curtis is turned away, unless he too has influence to use...his roll was lower, so he would need a level 5 favour to best Hercule.
Blastagarded 11/15/2020 12:43 PM
Hercule continues to stare bewildered at his cane
Narrator 11/15/2020 12:44 PM
Acting Commander Simond begins to sweat. "Ah, Major Sinnoch...yes, of course, your family name precedes you. Pedigree, indeed!" He extends a Major's commission letter to Curtis.
Curtis Sinnoch 11/15/2020 12:45 PM
Ah, very good, my man! It will be a pleasure following in my fathers footsteps!
Jules Lavelle 11/15/2020 12:46 PM
Wait a moment, sir.
12:49 PM
I may be a mere Captain in this regiment. But I have experience in leadership. And I can say, between these two, the Knight is the more reliable, the better acquainted with the integrity befitting a gentleman.
Narrator 11/15/2020 12:50 PM
Jules has used a level 3 favour to influence the commander in favor of Hercule.
Blastagarded 11/15/2020 12:53 PM
Hercule looks up from his cane in mild shock, pausing his mumblings of congratulations to Maj Sinnock and trying to process the events unfolding before him
Curtis Sinnoch 11/15/2020 12:54 PM
What is that, my good Lavelle? I understand you may have your opinions sir, and Good Hercule has certainly proved his place within the upper crust by deeds and actions - how has your own military "leadership" faired you this past year? I myself have been mentioned in dispatches no less then 3 times, and the stories of my valor are second to none!
12:55 PM
Using L3 favor - my last
12:55 PM
And may I mention that is my SL favor - not that of the mistress!
Blastagarded 11/15/2020 1:18 PM
The unfolding competition kicks Hercule's mind back into gear. He straightens his back and flourishes his cane striking on the floor to demand the room's attention Maj. Sinnoch, while no one would deny your accomplishments to France, I believe in your boasting you may have forgotten yourself a bit. I too have been mentioned no less than three times in dispatches and, as for valor... well... it is SIR Hercule, not "Good" Hercule for a reason. I understand that we both covet a Majority in this Regiment, but I take great pride in the day the King himself knighted me. I ask that you not forgot it or gloss over it in front of the Commander here. (OOC: Convert my two Level 2 Social influence into a Level 3)
2
Narrator 11/15/2020 1:21 PM
The two are currently tied. The tie will go to Hercule unless Curtis has more influence to spare.
1:22 PM
Or some other player takes an interest....
Curtis Sinnoch 11/15/2020 1:23 PM
Sir Bonaparte, you are correct regarding your title - my apologies for my slip up. It is a new title which you have fully earned, and in no way was I speaking against your merits - more of Lavelle here butting in
Blastagarded 11/15/2020 1:27 PM
Hercule gives a small nod of acknowledgement and hopes the display of asserting authority and command will be enough to sway the Commander's opinion
Curtis Sinnoch 11/15/2020 1:28 PM
(OOC: I am out, unless any other patrons decide to intercede)
Narrator 11/15/2020 1:29 PM
Simond slides the Major's commission across the table towards Hercule. "Er, sorry about the confusion, Sir Bonaparte!"
1:30 PM
Curtis, will you join the RFG as a Captain, or remain in your current regiment?
Blastagarded 11/15/2020 1:33 PM
A pride filled Hercule, graciously accepts the commission and salutes his new commander. He then takes his leave to go tell his love the good news. He continues to slightly flourish his cane on the way out, not fully understanding why it was important, but not willing to argue against the practice
1
Jules Lavelle 11/15/2020 1:36 PM
Hercule is now the acting commander of the 1st Battalion of the RFG
Curtis Sinnoch 11/15/2020 1:37 PM
I will remain with my current Regiment as it were, as the position I was interested as been taken by Sir Bonaparte, who has proven himself in the field of battle. @Blastagarded I wish to congratulate you on this command - I'm sure it will be in good hands
1:37 PM
@Jules Lavelle - I wish to have words with you outside, cur
Narrator 11/15/2020 3:31 PM
The Sinnochs have sent a messenger to the Minister of War's office pointing out the great service their family has offered the military in the past. The next day, Acting Commander Germain Simond is summarily dismissed and sent to the Frontier Regiments for his shameful mishandling of the application process.
3:32 PM
Curtis has been given the rank of junior Major. The Minister of War attempts to have him appointed Acting Regimental Commander, but the King intervenes, insisting the commander of the Royal Foot Guards must bear a title in his own right.
3:34 PM
Hercule is now the Acting Commander of the Royal Foot Guards.

Blastagarded 11/20/2020 2:33 PM

Jules Lavelle 11/20/2020 2:36 PM

1
2
Blastagarded 11/20/2020 6:45 PM
Excellent, Capt. @Jules Lavelle. Having read correspondence from the front, I am underwhelmed by our progress. After these last few campaigns, France is in need of a grand victory to ensure neighboring nations do not begin to question our military might. Now is not the time for its greatest fighting force to be sitting on the side lines. Please have the appropriate dispatches prepared and sent, advising the men to indulge themselves this October, for November we march to the aid of our countrymen.
Jules Lavelle 11/20/2020 6:46 PM
Yes, sir!!

Ghôst Touchard 1/2/2021 10:51 AM
@Narrator, do we know who is being deployed for Spring yet?
Jules Lavelle 1/2/2021 11:39 AM
Will let you know shortly
11:45 AM
no players are affected by any NPC volunteers
Ghôst Touchard 1/11/2021 9:51 AM
@Jean Renault what say you to fighting for our country in May? There's too much rain in April to do much but loaf about and drink. By May we should be invigorated and our hearts will be overflowing with wine and patriotism!
Jules Lavelle 1/11/2021 9:54 AM
Look at this guy seeking advice from a...subaltern??? Such bonhomie in the Royal Marines; can't be good for morale.
Ghôst Touchard 1/11/2021 9:56 AM
This man is my senior in that he has battle experience with this regiment, I am still working my way through the ranks (admittedly at a frenzied pace). I am only just getting the measure of Paris, and I respect the interests of my men.
9:59 AM
Also, having just started a courtship myself with the lovely Angélique Beauchamp (and hearing sordid tales of the last time Jean was deployed), I wanted to allow him to have a month's warning in order to fully satisfy any curiosities his mistress might have in his absence
2
Ghôst Touchard 1/18/2021 10:13 PM
The Royal Marines intend to serve our great nation in May!
Narrator 1/18/2021 10:15 PM
you only command the 1st Battalion, but yeah you can volunteer it
Ghôst Touchard 1/18/2021 10:17 PM
Then yep, I volunteer the 1st Battalion
Jules Lavelle 1/18/2021 10:24 PM
@Blastagarded, the Royal Foot Guards' 2nd Battalion will be deploying, whether or not the regiment goes, though I do hope to serve once more under your inspiring leadership.
Blastagarded 1/18/2021 11:14 PM
@Jules Lavelle Of course the regiment shall be marching in full. I wouldn't have it any other way. We can have our mettle getting rusty, can we?

so yes, you are going

Jean Renault 1/26/2021 10:35 AM

10:37 AM

Jean Renault 1/26/2021 10:37 AM

10:38 AM

Blastagarded 2/25/2021 2:08 PM
Is my understanding correct that I get a +1 bonus on the roll for each SL I am above the minimum SL? That's what the rules I have say, but I don't know if they differ from yours. In this case: minimum SL5, my SL12 = +8 bonus.
Narrator 2/25/2021 2:30 PM
It's +1 for each 3 SL over the minimum
2:31 PM
so yeah you get a +2
2:31 PM
you're welcome to roll it at 5 or use influence

It's +1 for each 3 SL over the minimum

2:11 PM
As commander, you may choose whether players who apply to the regiment are accepted or not (they no longer roll). Also, when volunteering, you may choose to volunteer the regiment along with you, so you get the benefit of the command on campaign. You may also choose to send all or any portion of your command volunteering with you.

Abelard Soucy 8/8/2021 6:01 PM

Narrator 8/8/2021 7:07 PM
@CitrusNotes, you asked to join the Royal Marines, but they are currently on campaign, along with all regiments but the 69th Arquebusiers and the Gascon Regiment. Would you like to apply to one of these instead? After the summer campaign, you will have many more options.
CitrusNotes 8/8/2021 7:28 PM
Certainly, I must have seen an outdated enlistment notice and sent my interest to the wrong party. My apologies. I would like to apply to the most prestigious regiment that does not require a horse.
Narrator 8/8/2021 7:29 PM
the 69th, then. you need a 3
CitrusNotes
used /roll
Dice Golem BOT 8/8/2021 7:30 PM

7:30 PM

CitrusNotes 8/8/2021 7:31 PM

Narrator 8/8/2021 7:31 PM

used /roll
Dice Golem BOT 8/8/2021 7:35 PM

Narrator 8/8/2021 7:35 PM
You have been accepted! Would you like to join as a Private, or purchase a higher rank?
CitrusNotes 8/8/2021 7:36 PM

Narrator 8/8/2021 7:36 PM
Congratulations to the most recent Private in the Gascon Regiment!
7:37 PM
as a private in the GR, you will receive 2 crowns per month pay, and 0 status points. you are required to perform 2 weeks per month of regimental duties (beginning next month)
7:37 PM
you can request a week off from these duties at the beginning of the month (before putting in your orders)
7:37 PM
you'll have to roll to see if the captain is feeling generous
Narrator 8/10/2021 12:33 AM
@Blastagarded, roll to join the Gascon Regiment. You need a 3.
Blastagarded 8/10/2021 12:39 AM
Sebastien enters what he thinks is a skills competition, not realizing it is a recruitment evaluation
Blastagarded
used /roll
Dice Golem BOT 8/10/2021 12:40 AM
1d6: (2) = 2
Narrator 8/10/2021 12:41 AM
The recruiter grimaces at the display. He wants to suggest a lower-status regiment to refer you to, but alas, there is none.
1
Blastagarded 8/10/2021 12:47 AM
Sebastien wanders away These city boys are tougher than I gave them credit for. But I have learned much and know what I have to practice now. Sebastien makes his way home to the communal stables, which he has mistaken for actual accommedations
Narrator 8/10/2021 12:49 AM
The grooms blithely fill the feed trough in Sebastien's stable without even looking inside. The mix of oats and vegetables appears not entirely unappetizing. From the next stall, he hears the chewing sounds of a horse enjoying its own meal.
Blastagarded 8/10/2021 12:52 AM
Sebastien marvels at the free food included in his accommodation Father was insane. "The city will eat you alive!" Ha! Paris is a wonder! Truly, everyone should live here.
Narrator 8/14/2021 11:23 PM
@Slade Imbee, due to your excellent standing in society, you are accepted in the Gascon Regiment without the frivolity of the usual application process.
11:24 PM
You may choose to start as a Private or purchase a higher rank, up to and including Major. You will need to purchase 1 horse as a Captain, and 3 horses as a Major.
Slade Imbee 8/15/2021 5:10 PM
While it would be my pleasure to serve at a higher rank, I regret to say that my current finances will not support horses. In due time I shall improve my equine companionship as well, but for now I must work my way up starting as subaltern
5:11 PM
err, actually, my finances don't support purchasing to subaltern either, so I fear I must begin as a private and prove my worth
Narrator 8/15/2021 6:48 PM

4:16 PM
@Ghôst Touchard, you may choose your appointment of Brigade Major from among the majors in the Royal Marines and Picardy Musketeers. "Ah, another resounding victory for us! I think I may investigate government work - my father suggested that it was fitting of my new title, though I find it dreadfully boring. As it happens, Felice's father has some contacts with the Minister of state, I wonder if he can assist me?"

An Interesting man, Primo. Many qualities both good and bad to consider.
4:53 AM

4:54 AM
Accept him

Narrator 9/13/2021 10:51 AM
@Abelard Soucy, would you like to purchase a commission?
Abelard Soucy 9/14/2021 9:17 PM
/Primo has been itching to move out of the 69th Arquebusiers since the moment he received his first pay and first distainful sniff from the noble ladies. However, he was stayed from action, first from the action on the summer campaign, then from the sudden and very frightening realization that he had courted his beau right out from under the nose of the officer running enlistment./
9:19 PM
/Knowing that a duel might very well be lurking for him at the Club, he lay low for the last month. Spending extra time with his regiment, as much as he detested their coarse manners./
9:22 PM
(He even skipped a meeting of the Chicken Fancier's Club, knowing he had developed a reputation of being a regular attendee, feigning illness and begging off from Armadine.)
Abelard Soucy 9/14/2021 9:31 PM
/Then, slowly, a new wind swept through the streets of Paris bringing relief to poor, cowering Primo. @Robert had found a new love. And in the blush of his amour, he had started to speak fondly of the 69th and give Primo credit for a battle he never fought in. Primo was certainly not about to disabuse him of that notion. He shined his shoes, straightened his cap, decorated it with a lucky feather from Armadine's cock, the Right Reverend Raphael Legoinegasque, and marched to the regimental house to apply and purchase a captaincy while the wind blew in his favor. /
9:32 PM
@Narrator purchase Captain
9:32 PM
@Joseph Bousain thank you, sir! I am certain that you will come to rely on me and my various skills soon!
9:34 PM
We're hatching a new clutch next month and we'd be honored if you accepted a gift of the finest of the brood!

used /roll
Dice Golem BOT 10/4/2021 8:07 PM
1d6: (4) = 4
Narrator 10/7/2021 10:21 PM
@Joseph Bousain requests to join the Royal Foot Guards. @Curtis Sinnoch, the choice is yours. (edited)
Curtis Sinnoch 10/8/2021 12:07 AM
hrm.. what do I know about the @Joseph Bousain family?
Curtis Sinnoch
hrm.. what do I know about the @Joseph Bousain family?
Joseph Bousain 10/8/2021 4:05 AM
Half of them are of excellent and ancient stock, the other half, not so much
Curtis Sinnoch 10/8/2021 7:58 AM
@Joseph Bousain what part of France do the 'ancient' stock hail from
7:58 AM
?
Curtis Sinnoch
?
Joseph Bousain 10/8/2021 9:10 AM
Monsieur, you insult me! To pretend you have not heard of the Earl of Amboise. Though I may not be his son by law or by his favour, the martial blood of his dynasty flows in my veins. The Amboise, who fought against the Protestants in the late civil war. Who fought the dread armies of Charles V and before them the cowardly English bowmen!
Curtis Sinnoch 10/8/2021 10:27 AM
Ah, but of course! A Bastard - how charming! Mayhaps some grandure does flow through you? Or, I gather, it may have flown right out of your mother. Regardless, you do have an air about you that I do appreciate... certainly more then some of this riffraff. I'll tell you what, @Joseph Bousain - you are welcome to join us as a private. Impress me upon the field of battle, and I'll even purchase a Subaltern position for you. Duty and discipline taught within the low ranks will do well for you, I think. If you can't handle it, well, I suppose you will just /wash out/
1
Narrator 10/8/2021 10:28 AM
(to be clear, Curtis can only decide whether to accept you or not -- once accepted you are free to purchase any position that's available through major [in this case, subaltern or captain])
Curtis Sinnoch 10/8/2021 10:31 AM
(Agreed - Curtis is offering to pay the Subaltern position after a battle with mention)
Joseph Bousain 10/8/2021 10:31 AM
[I think i can only go up to subaltern? I only have sl 8]
1
Narrator 10/8/2021 10:32 AM
correct
Curtis Sinnoch 10/8/2021 10:43 AM
I do recall the Earl, as well. I believe he has served my father on one or two occasions, mostly ferrying messages and other work the Viscount could not be bothered with. I'll be certain to mention as much in my next letter to the family estate.
Jules Lavelle 10/9/2021 1:29 PM
@Joseph Bousain waiting to see if you're buying subaltern or just entering as private
Joseph Bousain 10/9/2021 1:43 PM
I think I shall remain a private for now. Father always said one should know one's place

used /roll
Dice Golem BOT 11/21/2021 1:00 PM
1d6 enlist: (2) = 2
Abelard Soucy 11/21/2021 1:01 PM
After leaving the recruiting tent, Anton wanders around, looking for some foot soldiers and a card game.
Narrator 11/21/2021 3:42 PM
Rejected even by the Gascon Regiment. There is nowhere lower to apply.
Curtis Sinnoch 11/21/2021 6:24 PM
@Joseph Bousain - why do you wish to join the Royal Foot Guards? What drives you, in your desire to protect these glorious lands and the royal family itself?
Joseph Bousain 11/22/2021 7:23 AM
Monsieur, for many years I have devoted myself to the study of books and laws. I have left the fighting of wars to others. But now I have seen the field of battle, and though it is a terrible thing, I cannot take my mind away from the glory to be found there in the service of France. I understand at last why soldiers boast of their deeds on the field and I wish to share in their boasts!
Curtis Sinnoch 11/22/2021 9:25 AM
Ah man of letters, are you? Books and laws are all fine, but understanding military history - such is the true value of the written word! To read orders and missives, to understand what is happening both at home and abroad, is a mark of true command! @Joseph Bousain - we will gladly take you and your letters. Be prepared - any cowardly behavior will be dealt with extreme prejudice - we shall not sully the honor of France!
2

@Joseph Bousain, purchasing a commission?

@Joseph Bousain, purchasing a commission?
